books.description: Rat loves to play hopscotch but he has no one to play with. His neighbour, Goat, is at home but he prefers to be alone. Can Rat persuade Goat to play with him?

books.title: Mother, Call that Bird!
books.description: A little child asks her mother to help her adopt a bird.

books.title: Beloved Daughter
books.description: Natabo has run away from her home! Do you know why?

books.title: Tiger, Tiger, Where Are You?
books.description: A tiger researcher is out looking for tiger hair and poop as part of his study. He finds plenty of signs of the tiger, but where is the big cat? After all, there are so many  ways to see a tiger without seeing a tiger.

books.title: Bobo and the Worms
books.description: Worms are chewing up plants. Bobo is on a mission to stop them. Can she manage it?

books.title: Gudugu Thadugu
books.description: Muni the mason has to break a shed and make a new clinic. He goes up the hill asking various people to help him. But sometimes help takes on an unexpected form!

books.title: A Camel for Kelam
books.description: Pabu’s niece Kelam wants a camel for her birthday. So he sets out from Rajasthan for Lanka to look for one. Find out where this journey takes him.

books.title: Thato's Birthday Surprise
books.description: It is one week until Thato’s Birthday. His mama tells him he will have a big surprise. What will it be?

books.title: The Tree
books.description: The tale of how a tree grows from a tiny seed.

books.title: Plek Plok, the Lucky Little Fish
books.description: Mommy and Plek Plok, her little son are swimming in shallow waters in the sea. Plek Plok sees bigger fish playing happily in the deep waters and wants to join them. Mommy warns he is too young to explore the deep waters, but Plek Plok goes anyway. What happens to him?

books.title: I Spy a Lake!
books.description: Manju and Hamid are playing a game of I Spy by the lake. But why is the game making Hamid sad? And what can they do about it? A story about pollution and the declining state of water bodies.

books.title: Sringeri Srinivas's Cow Must Moo!
books.description: Sringeri Srinivas is back with a new problem. His cow has stopped mooing and he is very angry. The farmer frowns at her. His children feed her sweets. But the cow does not moo. Join this lovable farmer's family and his new cat Manuli, in this tale with a twist.

books.title: Kaushik, the Kind Detective
books.description: With his new torch and magnifying glass, Kaushik feels like a true detective. Join him as he explores the forest and makes exciting new discoveries.

books.title: Where is My Mother?
books.description: A small baby bird is separated from its mother. The baby bird searches and searches for its mother but can’t find her. What will happen?

books.title: I Can Smell...
books.description: On a winter afternoon in Shantiniketan, Mini smells something delicious and decides to follow her nose. Follow Mini and find out what surprise awaits her.

books.title: When We Are Home
books.description: Home is the sights, the sounds, the smells and the touch of every room. In this book, Moromi remembers her home that was displaced by a flood.

books.title: Uh-oh
books.description: What happens when you get too close to some animals and plants? They use all sorts of tricks to protect themselves from danger. Find out in this hilarious book.

books.title: Binti Knows Her Mind
books.description: You can try telling Binti that she can’t do something, but she knows best!

books.title: A Girl Called Cheeku
books.description: It's just an ordinary day for Cheeku and her friends. But then something extraordinary happens. Will Cheeku save the day? A story that celebrates individuality and reminds us that each one of us is special.

books.title: The Story of Stories
books.description: In a happy, happy, happy forest lives Journalist Bear who is sad because he can't find a 'BREAKING NEWS' story. A Journalist Bear without a 'BREAKING NEWS' story, can you imagine! Then along comes Singing Sardarji who helps Bear see who he really is. Read this story which celebrates the power and magic of stories, and the people who weave them into being.

books.title: Pehelwaan ji Plays Cricket
books.description: The children of the village were scared of the big man, Pehelwaan ji. So when he wanted to play with them, they just had to let him play.

books.title: Pehelwaan Ji
books.description: Pehelwaan Ji was a bit of a bully. But Gappu soon took care of that. Read this amusing story about their bout of wrestling!

books.title: Avani and the Pea Plant
books.description: Who did it? Avani does not know. Her mother does not know. But you will know who did it when you read this book about a pea plant.

books.title: Kaka and Munni: A Folktale from Punjab
books.description: Kaka, the wicked crow, wants to eat Munni's eggs. But Munni is a very clever sparrow. And so are all the characters in this popular folktale from Punjab.

books.title: Brave Rima
books.description: Rima is excited to visit the Boishakhi fair with her uncle. But while she is busy looking at all the wonderful things around her, she realizes her uncle is gone. Rima is lost! Will Rima be able to find her uncle in such a huge crowd?

books.title: Aiman's School Bag
books.description: After you read this story, you may wonder what your school bag feels about you. Aiman and her bag make quite a pair!

books.title: The Big Book of Boochandis
books.description: Beware! Boochandis can be anywhere and can give you a bit of a fright. Beloved bogeymen of Tamil Nadu, boochandis are summoned to get unwilling children to do as they are told. In this book are boochandis of all shapes and sizes, boochandis who growl and prowl. Watch out!

books.title: Hot Tea and Warm Rugs
books.description: Meenu's smart winter uniforms are out and the air is filled with the aroma of roasted nuts. Not to forget a cloud of mist in front of her mouth while she speaks! Let us find out what is happening this winter.

books.title: Our Friends in the Wild
books.description: Throughout time, people have shared a strong relationship with animals in nature. This non-fiction poem explores the many ways that animals help humans.

books.title: Listen to My Body
books.description: Each of us has a wonderful body. But have you ever listened to what it tells you?

books.title: Ritu's Letter Gets Longer!
books.description: Little Ritu is very eager to see her cousin. So she posts him a very special letter.

books.title: Smart Sona Helps Her Mother
books.description: Sona is smarter than ever. You may have read how she helped her father. This time she finds her mother’s work very interesting!

books.title: The Special Painter
books.description: Teo is missing the fingers on his right hand, but he loves painting and hopes to become a painter. Do you think Teo will be able to live out his dream in art class?

books.title: Aunty Jui's Baby
books.description:
Anu and her mother visit Aunty Jui to see her new baby. 
Aai and Aunty fuss over her but Anu thinks the baby is really silly.

books.title: With You, Ma
books.description: Aadya refuses to do anything without her Ma - whether it’s packing a bag for their holiday to Assam or it’s playing in the river. Will this trip make Aadya more self-reliant?

books.title: Rose and Rocky Go Green
books.description: Rose has a recipe to turn waste into plant food. Join Rose and Rocky in their adventures in this book about composting.

books.title: Hungry on the Steps
books.description: Pranav is hungry. As he climbs up the steps to his house slowly, many others join the line behind him. Who can make him run up the steps?

books.title: Sports Day
books.description: Lace up your shoes, drink some water, and get ready for Sports Day. Happy, sad, nervous, excited - the students of KV School are feeling so many emotions. Aska-lakadi-gala-galoo, cheer them, won’t you?

books.title: Manasa Collects a Rainbow
books.description: Manasa is on the hunt for a different kind of rainbow. Can she do it? Let’s find out. Join Manasa on her adventure.

books.title: Emotions Come and Go
books.description: Look out for the emotions you feel and why!

books.title: The Adventures of Akara and Linio
books.description: Akara is a standing line. Linio is a sleeping line. The two friends go out to play with their other line friends. What do you think happens?

books.title: Colourful Birds
books.description: Once upon a time, all the birds in the world were white. Only Grandfather Magic could change their colours. What did he create?

books.title: All About Beaks
books.description: Birds have beaks of all kinds, and they use them in many different ways. Dip in.

books.title: Sahana Makes Music
books.description: Sahana’s home is too quiet. How can Sahana liven it up? She starts creating some music of her own. Listen in.

books.title: Croak
books.description: There’s a frog in the classroom, warn the students. A frog is eating up masterji’s lunch. A frog is sitting on masterji’s chair. But masterji isn’t going to fall for his students’ pranks today.

books.title: It's a Laddoo Party!
books.description:
Mira and Mihir have a box full of laddoos. Just when they want to eat… DING DONG!
Oh, a guest. They have twelve laddoos.
How many laddoos do they each get?

books.title: Mr. Anand has an Adventure
books.description: Mr. Anand loves to go walking in new places. Every outing is an adventure to him. This time he thinks he has learnt a new language. Do you also think so?

books.title: Samira's Awful Lunch
books.description: Samira doesn't like the lunch her mother has packed in her tiffin box. All her animal friends feel bad for her and offer her their lunch. Read all about Samira's lunchtime adventure...

books.title: Amma, There is a Bat in My Sambar!
books.description:
Suppi is not at all happy to see a bat flying about in his house. In fact, he is quite scared of the bat.
How can Suppi and Amma get the bat back to her family?

books.title: Little Monkey's Lost Bananas
books.description: Little Monkey’s home is always messy. One day, Little Monkey feels hungry but his house is so messy that he cannot find his bananas to eat! He asks the other animals in the forest if they've seen his bananas. Do you think the other animals can help Little Monkey find his lost bananas?

books.title: The Doll That Bommakka Made
books.description: This charming cumulative tale celebrates the tradition of hand-crafted dolls, inspired by the South Indian Thanjavur Thalai Atti Bommai. Starting with a little ball of clay, we see the fine dancing doll grow before our eyes. The rhythmic descriptive words, stacked in a delightfully repetitive way, invite children to chant along. The vibrant illustrations complete the vivid imagery.

books.title: An Adventure with a Water-Snake
books.description: Khan catches a water snake! In this underwater adventure, Khan meets strange creatures, makes new friends and even manages to save the day.

books.title: Daddy's Mo
books.description: What does Anu like the most about her dad? His Mo! Do you know what a Mo is? Actually, Anu likes everyone with a Mo. So much so that when she sees a Mo, she gets funny ideas!

books.title: How Far is Far?
books.description: If you thought your friend's house on the other side of town was far away, you have clearly not read this book. Climb the Magic Math Ladder to get from where you are to the top of Mount Everest, to Kashmir, to the moon, the Sun, and ultimately, to the edge of the Universe, which is very, very, VERY far away indeed. Ready, steady, go!

books.title: The Magic Letter
books.description: Ijas receives a letter from his best friend Vikram. He can't wait to read it! But when he opens the letter, he is in for a surprise.

books.title: Rainbow Girls
books.description: Are all girls alike? Do they like the same things, should they all dress similarly? Let's think about it.

books.title: The Bone Puzzle
books.description: Zoya and Armaan have made a mess at Ma’s clinic. They will be in BIG trouble if she finds out. Can they put aside their differences and work as a team?

books.title: Book of Bhoots
books.description: Who whispers your name in the wind on this moonless night? Maybe nothing. Maybe something. Turn the pages of this book, and maybe you’ll hear it. And if you do, there’s no going back.

books.title: The Time-Travelling River
books.description:
Imagine all the stories rivers could tell us about the past, present and future!
Rivers were there when humans wanted to grow food. They were there when humans started to build cities. They are still here, providing water, food and livelihoods. But they are also being treated like rubbish bins. Rivers know how to take care of us, but if we want a future with them, maybe it is time for humans to take care of our precious water bodies.

books.title: The Power of One
books.description: The children of Class 6 want to visit the amusement park, but will they be able to save enough money for the tickets?

books.title: Vira and Tilli
books.description:
Vira has been looking after Tilli, and now it’s harvest time. Has Tilli grown into a healthy onion? When the time comes, will he be able to
let go of Tilli? A heartwarming story about a boy and his onion plant, and how our food is grown.

books.title: Dipa Karmakar - In Perfect Balance
books.description:
Dipa Karmakar was the first Indian female
gymnast to compete in the Olympic Games.
In 2016, she finished fourth at the event held in Rio. She grew up in Tripura and began learning gymnastics at the age of five. Read about her journey from Agartala to Rio.

books.title: Mukhil's Bloody Adventure
books.description: When Mukhil gets a fever, her mother takes her to a clinic. Give Mukhil company as she goes through her very first blood test.

books.title: Why Don't Birds Comb Their Hair?
books.description: Some birds have hair that sticks out in funny ways. What would you do if you had hair like that? Read this book to meet some fascinating birds.

books.title: Sizing Things Up
books.description: The world is made up of many things — big, small and middling. A small book with a big message.

books.title: Why Can't We Glow Like Fireflies?
books.description: Did you know that fireflies are also called lightning bugs? And did you know that baby fireflies use their glow to chase away predators? Jump right into this book if you want to find out more about these creatures that light up the dark.

books.title: Chuchu Manthu's Jar of Toffees
books.description: Chuchu Manthu is the most loving person Preet knows. After his death, Preet wonders if his kindness has disappeared with him. Based on a true story about loss and grief, and compassion in everyday actions.

books.title: Mini's First Vote
books.description: Mini is in Standard 6, old enough to vote in the school elections. Soon, she has to help choose the school captain. Whom should she vote for?

books.title: Razia Learns to Swim
books.description: Razia must learn to swim. But she’s terrified of water! Join Razia in the lagoon as she kicks, wiggles and floats.

books.title: Every Minute Counts
books.description: Adil has a full hour to get ready and walk to school. But he is late every day! Where is he losing time? His friend Paro decides to help him account for every minute of the hour.

books.title: The Auto That Flew
books.description: Caught in the mad traffic of Delhi, an auto feels a touch of magic. Take a ride with the driver and the passenger, and feel the feather touch of a little stardust.

books.title: Every Rupee Counts
books.description: Banko has saved some money. He wants to donate the money to people who rescue animals. Daee helps him convert paise to rupees and teaches him how to count the value of money.

books.title: Why are Bubbles Round?
books.description: Maya and Manu want to see if they can make bubbles of different shapes. Like them, you too can learn about bubbles and their shapes.

books.title: At Least I'm Okay!
books.description: Una, a mountain goat, lives on top of a mountain in beautiful Sakuland. The animals love their home but one day, the climate begins to change and the animals are forced to leave Sakuland. A story about climate change and how it affects animals and the natural world.

books.title: Shruthi Floats an Idea
books.description: Shruthi has to grow a plant. But she doesn’t want to grow it in soil. Is there any other way?

books.title: Why Do Sunflowers Love the Sun?
books.description: All Viveka wants to do is paint the perfect picture of the sunflower field next to her house. But why won’t the flowers stay still?

books.title: Who's on  Divya's Map?
books.description: Divya is excited that her cousin Ravi is going to come to Gajapur soon. But she is not sure how he will find her house. Would a map help?

books.title: A Friend for Little Goat
books.description: Little Goat does not really like going to school. The school is so big, and Little Goat is so little. But he discovers that friends make school a happier place.

books.title: Gola Gola
books.description: Everyone wants a different-coloured gola from the mela! If only there were an easier way for Mariyu to remember all of their choices? A story that introduces the concept of Venn diagrams.

books.title: How to Solve a Problem like Himani
books.description:
In a tiny school in the Uttarakhand hills,
Himani and her classmates learn how to
solve real-life problems. The story takes
you through her diary entries as she learns
the steps to solve problems, big and small.

books.title: Suppamma Loves to Craft
books.description: Suppamma loves to build things out of recycled waste. But she doesn’t like paying attention in school, or doing homework. Can Suppamma become successful by following her passions?

books.title: Piku's Little World
books.description:
Piku loves long summer afternoons, and
dislikes dark summer evenings. But his
afternoons become darker when, all of
a sudden, there is no one to read Piku a
story. Will Piku’s books remain unread?

books.title: Achchachi's Bagbrella
books.description: Sahyadri lost her umbrella at school, again. Amma is going to be very angry. Luckily for Sahyadri, Achchachi is going to help her make a new umbrella. A story that introduces the concept of pie charts.

books.title: A Tea Garden Party
books.description: Abha is going to the cool hills of Darjeeling. On the way she stops at a tea factory. She has so many questions! Is tea a leaf? Where does it grow? What makes it taste so good? Go on a tour of a tea garden and factory and find the answers to these questions with Abha.

books.title: Let's Fly a Plane!
books.description: Have you ever dreamed of being a pilot? Open this book, step into the cockpit and let’s fly an aeroplane!

books.title: Up World, Down World
books.description: In a canopy forest in India, the Up World and Down World have always been suspicious of each other. But it all changes when Gopa the dormouse drops her book by accident on Fatima’s head. A story about the biodiversity of the canopy forests and friendship.

books.title: Sweet Memories of Gutar Goo
books.description: Open this special album to meet Gutar Goo and his rocking family and friends. Join the pigeon as he steps out of his (egg)shell into a wonderful new world where he finds his flock and is embraced with love and joy. A tongue-in-cheek book that celebrates identity and of course, pigeons.

books.title: Every Drop Counts
books.description: Samir is on a mission to save all the water he can. His mission takes him to several houses where he learns new and exciting ways to make every drop count! Can he, with the help of his Mina Chachi, make a difference?

books.title: That Night
books.description:
Chaitu wakes up to loud and angry voices. The night is dark but a fire rages in the distance. Chaitu is terrified for her family, friends and home. Why are these voices so angry? What do they want? A story set in a world fed by fear
and intolerance.

books.title: Slip and Slide
books.description: The hills are covered with snow. Seven sisters are excited to go out and play. Join them on their adventure!

books.title: The Day the Vegetables Came to School
books.description: Raju cannot believe his eyes. On his way to school, he sees vegetables of all shapes and sizes also headed there, carrying their schoolbags and water bottles! What’s more, no one else, including his teacher, seems surprised to see them. Whatever is going on?

books.title: How Heavy Is Heavy?
books.description: A stone is heavier than a feather and lighter than a rock. So is the stone heavy, or is it light? Can we know for sure how heavy (or light) a stone is? Find answers to these and other fascinating questions in this fun book about weight.

books.title: Rainbow Boys
books.description: Are all boys alike? Do they like the same things, should they dress similarly? Let's think about it.

books.title: The Very Shocking Report Card
books.description:
What would happen if report cards were not all about studying and writing exams? What
marks would you get then?

books.title: Think Before You Act
books.description: A man takes advice from his friend without thinking twice about it himself. Where do you think this will lead him?

books.title: A Dancer's Tale
books.description: Phyllis Spira was dancing by the age of four. That was just the beginning of her journey towards becoming one of South Africa’s Prima Ballerinas.

books.title: Apu's Giant Earthquake
books.description: Did Apu’s singing cause the earth to tremble and shake? Or was it an earthquake? Find out more in this humorous and fascinating story about earthquakes.

books.title: Why Do Bees Buzz?
books.description: Why can't bees work quietly? They buzz because there are some very good reasons to do so. Learn about the humble bees in this book.

books.title: The Missing Bat
books.description: There was a cricket bat missing from Rehman Chacha's warehouse, and he suspected that young Ahmed had stolen it. A funny tale from Kashmir, the land of the willow trees.

books.title: My Brother's Wheeeeelchair
books.description: Meet Durva and Dhrubo. And Dhrubo's wheeeeelchair. Join the brother and sister as they explore parks, markets, and many other places and have lots of fun together, just like siblings do.

books.title: Reeti and Mithu
books.description: Reeti's holidays have begun, but she isn't happy. She doesn't like holidays because she misses being with her friends. One day she gets a pet - Mithu, the talking parrot. Does Mithu become her new best friend? Read this heartwarming story of friendship to find out!

books.title: So Many Leaves
books.description: Some leaves are tiny, others are not. Some taste sweet, others bitter. There are so many different kinds of leaves around us. Leaf through the leaves of this book about leaves.

books.title: A Big Day for the Little Wheels
books.description: The little wheels in Shazia’s new cycle think they are very special. What they don’t know is that wheels are everywhere, even when we can’t see them.

books.title: Tree's Company: a play
books.description:
Three stories about three special trees. A
wonderful play about the importance of
plants in our lives, with a strong message
of conservation.

books.title: You Won't Believe Me
books.description: Here is a boy who likes his school a lot, but is sure that his school does not like him back. His classmates bully him. His teachers shout at him. But there is a pond full of fish — where he discovers that he’s not alone.

books.title: Ahilyabai Holkar
books.description:
This is the story of Ahilyabai Holkar, a queen who ruled Malwa for 28 years. Read about
this ruler who made history with her deeds.

books.title: With Love, Gaia
books.description:
A beautiful leaf flutters from the palm of Gaia, Mother Earth, with a message for Kaya and Kabir. 
What’s the message?
Read on to find out about Kaya and Kabir’s adventure.

books.title: Bonda and Devi
books.description: Do best friends always have to be alike? Devi and Bonda are best friends, but Devi is a little girl, while Bonda is a… Well, he can lift heavy boxes, he can extend his arms and legs, he never forgets anything he’s told, he can be turned on and off. Can you guess what he is?

books.title: Monumental Maths
books.description:
What do the pyramids, ancient temples
and Jantar Mantar all have in common?
Maths! Innovative architects across the
world have used mathematics to build
wonders that we still marvel at today.

books.title: Full of Light
books.description: When it comes to her Amma, Shakthi can’t seem to do anything right. She feels like a disappointment—until she meets a girl who reminds her that all of us have the ability to shine bright.

books.title: Kallu's world 3 - Mangu Mali and the Ambia Bhoot
books.description:
Welcome to Khajuria – a village where young Kallu and his gang run delightful adventurous riots almost everyday. Sometimes they question the village traditions, defy bullies or might just be going about their daily business but Kallu and his gang are up for anything. Join them as they grow wiser and wittier, happily roaming the village finding new things to do, and see what they come up with!
Everybody in Khajuria knew about Munia's temper. Everybody also knew how stingy Mangu Mali was about giving away mangoes from the tree in his orchard. Now, Munia wanted mangoes...really badly and that too from Mangu Mali's orchard. Can you imagine a situation like this? Read on, to find out what happens...

books.title: What Happens When You Flush?
books.description: Nozy the pet rat finds himself in the underground sewers. It’s a good thing Cheeky is around to take him on a splashy, smelly adventure!

books.title: At Home
books.description: Ammini misses school, and her brother Unni misses his aunt. Both of them miss playing in the park. But everyone has to stay indoors. Maybe they can go out for a walk today? Ammini hopes so. A day in the life of two children in a time of lockdowns and social distancing.

books.title: Moonlight in the Sea
books.description: Fatima grows up on an idyllic island in the Lakshadweep, watched over by Nihla, the moon. She falls in love with the underwater world of colourful corals, fascinating fish, mysterious manta rays, grizzled old turtles and dancing dolphins. One day, in a terrible storm, she gets lost at sea. Join Fatima on her incredible adventure on remote islands with wonderful marine creatures.

books.title: Everyday Superheroes
books.description: Superheroes don’t always wear capes, or carry weapons. They don’t always fly. Some superheroes wear masks and protective suits. Some carry stethoscopes and thermometers. And some look through microscopes and examine lab reports. A book on everyday heroes in the time of a pandemic.

books.title: Jungle Brew
books.description: Bulbuli had heard so much about the mysterious place in the jungle that everybody talked about but nobody had visited. Was she actually going to have the biggest adventure of her life with only Totaram for company?

books.title: Raza Meets the King
books.description: Raza's father Rahmat Khan is the royal tailor to none other than the great Mughal emperor Akbar. When they go to the palace to deliver the Emperor's new summer clothes, Raza realizes that the emperor is not completely satisfied with his father's designs. Raza wants desperately to help his father. But what can a little boy do?

books.title: The Body Builders
books.description: Vava is amazed that his little sister knew how to make her hair, her lungs and her ever-growing legs when she was in Amma’s belly. Join him as he explores how the millions of cells inside us grow and build our bodies.

books.title: Under the Invisibility Cloak
books.description: What would you do if you had an invisibility cloak? For one, playing hide-and-seek would be great fun. But is anyone making invisibility cloaks? Or do they exist only in science fiction and fantasy stories? Arnav and Tanisha head to the library to find out. Let’s follow them quietly – they can’t see us, we are invisible in their world.

books.title: Walking in the Wild
books.description: Take a walk with Zakhuma, a brave forest guard who lives in Mizoram. As Zakhuma takes us through his forest, we learn about the different animals and what it means to protect the jungle.

books.title: Who Got the Goat?
books.description: Chinnasura and Cheekasura spot a stray goat. Both want it. The only way out is to have a neer-more drinking contest. Learn fractions and decimals while the asuras drink up.

books.title: Jackpot
books.description: Vikram has high and lofty dreams. He wants to travel the world like a prince and build a library that's unparalleled in its glory. But Vikram can't be bothered with working hard to earn his desires. He looks for the easy way to attain his dreams, and hence proceeds to buy a lottery ticket. Does he win the lottery and finally travel the world? Or does his life begin to crumble due to his thirst and greed for easy money?

books.title: The Salt Inspector
books.description: Munshi Vanshidar lacks the ability to be dishonest or corrupt in spite of being surrounded by greedy people soaked in money. As the salt inspector, he is true to himself and to the integrity of his work. Soon, this leads him to clash with Pandit Alopideen, the most powerful man in their area. What follows is a tale that will always be relevant to our society, portraying the constant war between the lure of money and the need to be moral.

books.title: Cracking the Code: Women Who Have Changed the Way We Look at Computers
books.description: What do 19th Century English mathematician Ada Lovelace, new-age fashion designer Anouk Wipprecht, and the current Digital Minister of Taiwan Audrey Tang have in common? They’ve all impacted the way we look at computers and coding. This book introduces some inspiring women from the world of technology, from across a century.

books.title: Pick It Up
books.description: A story that takes a stance against bullying and emphasizes on doing the right thing.

books.title: Sir M. Visvesvaraya: The Builder of Dams, Bridges and a Nation
books.description: As a child, Vishwa walked a long way to get to school. As a college student, he tutored two children to make ends meet. And as an engineer, he built dams, bridges and institutions, and the foundation for a modern India. Read this book to know how Sir M. Visvesvaraya observed the world around him, learnt from it, made bold decisions and never shied away from hard work.

books.title: Something's Stuck Around the Duck
books.description:
When Nandini and Kavitha go to the park, they find a lot of garbage lying around.
A duck has tried to eat a piece of plastic but now it is stuck around her beak.
Can the girls help save the duck and clean up the park for good?

Read on to find out!

books.title: Roy's Noisy Secret
books.description: Nobody must find out that Roy is wearing his grandmother’s tinkling anklet. But keeping a secret is harder than Roy imagined and he feels weighed down by guilt. How did Roy get himself into this mess and how will he find a way out?

books.title: Miss Bandicota Bengalensis Digs Up the Seashore
books.description:
Miss Bandicota Bengalensis was not just any little Indian mole rat. She had
magical eyes like no other mole rat and she wanted to see everything. Every
night, she packed her explorer's kit: her sunglasses, binoculars, magnifying
glass and of course, her favourite – a strip of sticker bandages. 'An explorer is
always prepared,' she said. Every time she dug in a new direction and returned
through a new burrow!
This time our unlikely explorer has surfaced near the sea. Enjoy a walk along
the beach with her as she befriends a host of strange creatures.

books.title: Polygon Family
books.description: The family of Polygons come together in an attempt to know each other! Do you want to know them?

books.title: We call her Ba - A Biography of Kasturba Gandhi
books.description:
Kasturba Gandhi was no ordinary woman. She had a strong identity, courage and tremendous willpower and she sacrificed a lot for her country. She was a friend and trusted companion of the Father of the Nation, Mahatma Gandhi. Gandhiji’s biographer, Louis Fischer wrote about her, 'Being herself and being at the same time a shadow of the Mahatma made her a remarkable woman.'
Read about this amazing woman who walked shoulder to shoulder with one of the most prominent leaders of the world during the most turbulent times of this peninsula.

books.title: Around You: States of Matter
books.description: Imagine you are at the local market. What do you see around you? Do you think all these things have something in common? A book that introduces readers to matter, and the properties of solids, liquids and gases.

books.title: Goal!
books.description: Every Sunday, Heena watches a group of girls playing football from her window. She wants to join them. But convincing Ammi and Abbu is not going to be easy. Will she be able to join the team? Run, kick and score goals and experience freedom like never before?

books.title: Miss Bandicota Bengalensis Discovers the Old Caves
books.description:
Miss Bandicota Bengalensis was no ordinary Indian mole rat. She was quite the adventurer. Every evening, she set out with her explorer's kit – sunglasses, binoculars, magnifying glass and a strip of sticker bandages.
'An explorer is always prepared,' she said. Through the night, she dug in a new direction, to a new sea, up a new hill...
Now, join Miss Bandicota and her new friend in exploring one of the oldest hills and caves of India.

books.title: My Mother's Child
books.description: My Mother’s Child is the story of Neo, a young boy who becomes a big brother. After being an only child all his life, how will Neo react to his new baby sister? Will his parents treat him differently? Neo is afraid he will become the outcast of the family. Read on to find out what happens!

books.title: My Elder Brother
books.description: The elder brother is found with books and formulas. The younger brother is found with butterflies and lost kites. The elder brother keeps studying everyday and barely does anything else. The younger brother plays in gardens and enjoys doing what he likes. The elder brother takes every chance to criticize his younger brother for not studying. The younger brother still keeps playing outdoors. What follows is a timeless tale of following one's own heart despite those that only ridicule and demotivate.

books.title: The Secrets of Rocks
books.description:
Rocks are everywhere — under the ground,
above the ground, and even at the bottom of
our oceans. But where do rocks come from?
What are they made of? If rocks could talk,
they would share many secrets.

books.title: The Adventures of Philautus Frog
books.description: Philautus, the little tree frog wants to see the wide blue sea, so far away from his home on Big Tree, in the middle of a forest. Take a magnificent trek with Philautus across the Western Ghats teeming with creatures that are funny, fat and fascinating. Seasoned with humour, this lush book reveals the splendours of the wild through a compelling tale.

books.title: Saru and her Little Secret
books.description: Mai does not approve of indiscipline and Saru finds it hard to always walk the straight and narrow path. Saru often gets into trouble with Mai. What has she done this time? What will happen when Mai finds out? Read on to find out more.

books.title: I Know My Rights
books.description: The United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child lists out the basic rights that children have. It doesn’t matter where you come from, what colour your skin is, whether you practice a religion or not, what language you speak, whether you are a boy or a girl or haven’t decided yet – you have the right to these rights! This poster books lists out some of the rights of the child.

books.title: The Art Gallery on Princess Street
books.description:
Kekoo and Khorshed Gandhy gave modern
Indian art a window to make a mark around
the world. Bombay’s Gallery Chemould was a
home away from home for the art community.

books.title: The World of Money
books.description: The Rupaiya Paisa Series brings out many useful facts about money that will help children to use money wisely as they grow up in an ever-changing world. What were early coins made of? How do people earn, save and use money? Can the family buy a fridge even if it does not have the money for it today? Find out all this and more in The World of Money, Book 1 in the Rupaiya Paisa Series.

books.title: How Zebra got his stripes
books.description: Brave Zebra saves the day.

books.title: B. R. Ambedkar: A life in books
books.description: This is the story of B. R. Ambedkar, also known as the Father of India’s Constitution. Babasaheb, as he was fondly called, was not only a visionary, an economist and a revolutionary, he was also a lifelong reader. This is the story of his love for learning and how it fuelled his journey.

books.title: Our Constitution, Our People
books.description: The Constitution of India is a dream, a vision, a set of promises. Step into the pages of Indian history to understand what this important document means for our present and our future, and what it means to be a citizen of this country.

books.title: Building a Building
books.description: Meenu and Elango are getting a new school! What goes into building one? Let's find out!

books.title: We All Need to Save Water
books.description: Baby bear Golu is taken to Rani Ki Vav, a famous stepwell in Gujarat, for a family trip. What does Golu learn from there?

books.title: Earth, Our Home
books.description: The story of Earth is the story of life. It is also a story of loss, one worsened by climate change. But while the climate crisis can be a story of devastation, the work being done to mitigate it, to adapt to it, brings hope. And reminds us, our future is in our hands. The verses in this book tell these stories, in the voices of the orangutan, the Hasdeo Arand forest, the Gangetic dolphin and the Pondicherry shark.

books.title: The Secret World of Mehlli Gobhai: The Man Who Found Art Everywhere
books.description: The murky waters of a winding stream, stark trees stripped of leaves, a crow messing up a canvas—Mehlli Gobhai saw art in all of this. Follow the journey of this inspiring painter and see how art is all around and can help you see the world anew.

books.title: Girls Also Want Azadi
books.description: Now girls also want azadi. Freedom from violence and harassment, the freedom to choose and to exercise their rights. And azadi to be who they want to be.

books.title: Where Did My Call Go?
books.description: When we make phone calls, our voice is able to travel to places that are far away. Have you ever wondered how cell phones can make a voice travel over long distances?

books.title: A Shade of GREEN
books.description: The air in the city is grey and polluted. Nayantara and Aliza feel suffocated. They dream of leaves that work day and night to produce oxygen and clean energy. Can they invent a leaf that does all that?

books.title: Grace: One engineer's fight to make science education accessible for all
books.description: Grace had many questions. Why did she have to sit outside her classroom? Why did she have to wear pants instead of a dress? Why could transgender persons not study engineering? She wondered why science and technology education was not accepting of differences in people. And she decided to find answers in the courtroom. This is the story of Grace Banu, who is working towards making science and technology education more accessible for all.

books.description:
አንድ አይን፣ ስድስት የእግር ጣት ያላት ጸጋ

ጸጋ ብርታት ያጣች አንድ አይን ስድስት የእግር ጣት ያላት ግመል ናት። ሁሌም ቢሆን እረኛዋን ካማድን ማስደሰት እና በሌሎች እንስሳቶች ተቀባይነት አግኝታ መኖርን ነበር የምትመኘው። ጸጋ የፈለገችውን ያህል ብትሞክርም ግን ክብ ቅርጽ እየሰራች ብቻ ነው መራመድ የምትችለው።

በዚህ መሃል…. አንድ መልካም የሆነ እንግዳ ሰው መጣ…

books.title: ນາງ ສຸກິດໄປໂຮງຮຽນ!
books.description: ນາງ ສຸກິດ ຕື່ນນອນຢ່າງຕື່ນເຕັ້ນໃນມື້ທີ່ນາງຈະໄປໂຮງຮຽນ. ມັນບໍ່ໄກທີ່ຈະຍ່າງໄປໂຮງຮຽນ, ແຕ່ນາງເຮັດບໍ່ໄດ້ເພາະວ່ານາງພິການແລະມີສາຍນ້ໍາທີ່ນາງຕ້ອງຜ່ານແລະເສັ້ນທາງທີ່ບໍ່ສະເຫມີກັນ. ​ແຕ່​ມື້​ໜຶ່ງ ອັບດຸນ, ເດັກ​ຊາຍ​ຈາກ​ໝູ່​ບ້ານ, ​ໄດ້​ເຮັດ​ວຽກ​ງານ​ເພື່ອ​ເຮັດ​ໃຫ້​ມັນ​ເປັນ​ໄປ​ໄດ້.

books.title: Ang Makatang Kaibigan
books.description:
Ang Makatang Bai (The Poetry Buddy) is a collection of satirical fiction that explores the humor and hidden truths of everyday life. Through witty and imaginative storytelling, it presents familiar situations with a playful twist inviting readers to laugh while recognizing the deeper realities beneath each moment.

As the collection unfolds, lighthearted scenes gradually give way to more thought-provoking themes, touching on behavior, society, and human nature. Blending entertainment with insight, this book offers more than just stories it leaves readers reflecting on the world around them, where every joke carries a truth worth noticing.

books.title: Le tambour de l'unité
books.description: Dans ce livre, les ethnies du Cameroun se rassemblent dans la savane du Nord pour partager leur chants et leurs récoltes. Les différends menacent de briser leur unité, mais les enfants, éveillés à la beauté du vivre-ensemble, refusent de céder à la peur. La hyène, symbole de division, tente de les intimider, mais leur chant, leur danse et leur solidarité la repoussent. Ainsi, malgré leurs différences de couleur et de culture, ils deviennent un seul peuple. Au matin, les parents découvrent leurs enfants actifs, porteurs d’une lumière nouvelle : celle de l’unité qui triomphe sur la division.

books.title: L’oiseau qui chantait la vérité
books.description: Un oiseau messager parcourt les villages, chantant des vérités oubliées. Son chant réveille les consciences et invite à la justice et à la paix.

books.title: Le Contraste des Destins
books.description: Le présent livre célèbre les fruits du respect et du travail. Trois enfants d'une même famille mais trois chemins. Deux choisissent l’obéissance, le respect et discipline et deviennent des modèles. Le troisième rejette les valeurs et sombre dans l’échec.

books.title: Célébration de la langue maternelle
books.description: ce livre relate le récit illustré de la célébration de la langue maternelle

books.title: La bonté du lièvre
books.description:
Dans la savane du Nord Cameroun, un lièvre sauve une pintade en danger.
Plus tard, ce geste de bonté lui sauvera la vie face à une hyène affamée.
Avec la sagesse du lion et du chameau, les animaux rappellent une grande vérité :
le bienfait revient toujours en bénédiction.

books.title: Choupi et Coucou
books.description:
Choupi, une petite souris grise, sort joyeusement dans la cuisine à la recherche de nourriture, sans se douter du danger.
Coucou, le chat noir, l’observe en silence et prépare son attaque avec patience et précision.
Pris par surprise, Choupi est capturée et dévorée, laissant Coucou satisfait de sa chasse réussie.

books.title: Lina et son chat Milo
books.description: Il s'agit dans ce livre de débutant l'histoire d'une fillette nommée Lina avec son chat Milo.

books.title: Djoboŋvunaŋ et le grand marché
books.description: Dans un village chaleureux du Cameroun dénommé Kotliina, Djoboŋvunaŋ et sa sœur Lumuda vivent des aventures pleines de joie, de foi et de sagesse. À travers les scènes du marché, les repas en famille et les chants de louange, ce livre célèbre l’éducation, le respect des parents, la générosité et la convivialité. Guidés par l’amour familial et la lumière de Dieu, les enfants deviennent des modèles pour leur entourage — une vraie fierté pour toute la communauté.

books.title: La découverte de la nature
books.description: il s'agit de l'histoire d'une petite fille se lève le matin et découvre les merveilles de la nature.

books.description: น้องวงกลมเป็นเด็กหญิงที่มีนิสัยขี้อาย แม้ว่าพ่อแม่และพี่ชวนไปไหนเธอก็ไม่ยอมไป จนเพื่อนๆเช่น น้องสามเหลี่ยม น้องสี่เหลี่ยมชนิดต่างๆรวมทั้งเพื่อนคนอื่นๆชวนไปเล่นด้วยน้องวงกลมจึงค่อยๆหายเขิน และสนุกสนานกับการกลิ้ง การกระโดดเล่น ครอบครัวของเธอก็พลอยมีความสุขไปด้วย

books.title: มาปลูกต้นไม้กัน...
books.description: โรงเรียนแห่งหนึ่งมีครูและเด็กๆซึ่งเป็นสัตว์ป่าที่น่ารักวันหนึ่งคุณครูชวนนักเรียนนำต้นกล้าไปปลูกเพื่อให้เป็นป่าที่ร่มรื่น เมื่อเด็กๆเหล่านี้เติบโตขึ้นต่างมาชื่นชมป่าไม้ที่เคยร่วมกันปลูกด้วยความรู้สึกภาคภูมิใจและมีความสุข

books.title: เสือกลับใจ
books.description: ในป่าแห่งหนึ่งที่มีความอุดมสมบูรณ์และมีเหล่าสัตว์มากมาย มีนกอยู่คู่หนึ่ง ได้ฟักไข่ และด้วยความกังวลของนกตัวผู้ที่กลัวว่าจะเกิดอันตรายกับไข่จึงคุยกับนกตัวเมียว่าจะไปขอร้องเสือเพื่อนรักให้ช่วยดูแลเฝ้าไข่ที่ฟักไว้ในรัง วันรุ่งขึ้นนักก็บินไปยังถ้ำเสือเพื่อขอความช่วยเหลือ เสือก็รับปาก  ในระหว่างที่เสือกับนกคุยกันอยู่นั้น

books.title: ชะนีตัวป่วน
